tossups.question: An unnamed book in this novel describes a burial on a snowy day and sparks a conversation that leads to the second request for a one-tenth payment. The business of a crystal merchant in this novel is boosted by cleaning the crystal and putting up a display case. The prologue of this novel recounts a version of the myth of (*) Narcissus where the lake mourns because it can no longer see its beauty reflected. A quote from this novel is "If you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you achieve it." The protagonist of this novel crosses a sea after having a recurring dream of a boy leading him to the pyramids. For 10 points, the shepherd Santiago goes on a journey in search of treasure in what novel by Paulo Coelho ("coe-EL-yo")?
tossups.question_sanitized: An unnamed book in this novel describes a burial on a snowy day and sparks a conversation that leads to the second request for a one-tenth payment. The business of a crystal merchant in this novel is boosted by cleaning the crystal and putting up a display case. The prologue of this novel recounts a version of the myth of (*) Narcissus where the lake mourns because it can no longer see its beauty reflected. A quote from this novel is "If you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you achieve it." The protagonist of this novel crosses a sea after having a recurring dream of a boy leading him to the pyramids. For 10 points, the shepherd Santiago goes on a journey in search of treasure in what novel by Paulo Coelho ("coe-EL-yo")?

tossups.question: This language was used to write a novel in which Ludo responds to an independence movement by barricading herself in her top-floor apartment. Another novel in this language set in a penthouse describes a perfectly clean room with a wall etching of a dog. The novels Sleepwalking Land and A General Theory of Oblivion were written in this language. This language was used to write the novel The (*) Passion According to G.H. In an international best seller written in this language, a young shepherd has a recurring dream of finding riches at the Pyramids. Clarice Lispector wrote in this language. Richard Zenith won the Pessoa Prize for translations from this language. For 10 points, name this language used by Paulo Coelho ("SEE-lo") to write The Alchemist.
tossups.question_sanitized: This language was used to write a novel in which Ludo responds to an independence movement by barricading herself in her top-floor apartment. Another novel in this language set in a penthouse describes a perfectly clean room with a wall etching of a dog. The novels Sleepwalking Land and A General Theory of Oblivion were written in this language. This language was used to write the novel The (*) Passion According to G.H. In an international best seller written in this language, a young shepherd has a recurring dream of finding riches at the Pyramids. Clarice Lispector wrote in this language. Richard Zenith won the Pessoa Prize for translations from this language. For 10 points, name this language used by Paulo Coelho ("SEE-lo") to write The Alchemist.

tossups.question: A character in this novel writes, "The Emerald Tablet is a direct passage to the Soul of the World" in the sand. After hearing a tourist complain of thirst in this novel, two men proclaim "maktub" and begin to sell tea from crystal glass in Tangiers. After being mugged by two thieves, the protagonist of this novel remembers a sycamore tree outside an abandoned church. An Englishman in this novel reveals Urim and Thummim, two black and white stones given to the protagonist by the King of Salem Melchizedek (mel-KEE-zeh-dek), are false omens. This novel's protagonist sells his sheep and leaves Andalusia after receiving a dream of treasure buried underneath the Pyramids. For 10 points, name this novel where Santiago discovers his Personal Legend, written by Paulo Coelho (PAW-loh koh-AY-loh).
tossups.question_sanitized: A character in this novel writes, "The Emerald Tablet is a direct passage to the Soul of the World" in the sand. After hearing a tourist complain of thirst in this novel, two men proclaim "maktub" and begin to sell tea from crystal glass in Tangiers. After being mugged by two thieves, the protagonist of this novel remembers a sycamore tree outside an abandoned church. An Englishman in this novel reveals Urim and Thummim, two black and white stones given to the protagonist by the King of Salem Melchizedek (mel-KEE-zeh-dek), are false omens. This novel's protagonist sells his sheep and leaves Andalusia after receiving a dream of treasure buried underneath the Pyramids. For 10 points, name this novel where Santiago discovers his Personal Legend, written by Paulo Coelho (PAW-loh koh-AY-loh).

tossups.question: A song directed at one holder of this political office accuses him of thinking that Paolo Coelho authored One Hundred Years of Solitude and features a chorus that translates to “weeds must be removed from the plantation.” In January 2020, a group of protestors carried a guillotine to the residence of the current holder of this political office, who is a member of the New (*) Progressive Party. That holder of this office, Wanda Vasquez, assumed it in August 2019 after its previous holder resigned amid a scandal involving the messaging app Telegram. The holder of this office requested that the United States waive the Jones Act in order to provide aid in the aftermath of Hurricane Maria. For 10 points, name this political office whose holders rule from San Juan.
tossups.answer: Governor of Puerto Rico (the song is Bad Bunny’s “Afilando los Cuchillos”, which criticized Gov. Ricardo Rosselló)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Governor of Puerto Rico (the song is Bad Bunny's "Afilando los Cuchillos", which criticized Gov. Ricardo Rossello)
tossups.question_sanitized: A song directed at one holder of this political office accuses him of thinking that Paolo Coelho authored One Hundred Years of Solitude and features a chorus that translates to "weeds must be removed from the plantation." In January 2020, a group of protestors carried a guillotine to the residence of the current holder of this political office, who is a member of the New (*) Progressive Party. That holder of this office, Wanda Vasquez, assumed it in August 2019 after its previous holder resigned amid a scandal involving the messaging app Telegram. The holder of this office requested that the United States waive the Jones Act in order to provide aid in the aftermath of Hurricane Maria. For 10 points, name this political office whose holders rule from San Juan.

tossups.question: In a work from this country, the image of the Emperor’s black beard is repeated in a poem that summarizes the desire of the virgin Ofenisia. An essay about the “transfiguration of taboo into totem” declares “Tupi or not Tupi, that is the question”; that is this country’s “Cannibal Manifesto” by Oswald de Andrade (“OHZ-vald jee ahn-DRAH-jee”). In a novel from this country, the Bastos (“BAH-stoose”) clan and Mundinho Falcão (“moon-JEEN-yoo fao-COW”) struggle for control of the town of Ilhéus (“eel-YEH-oos”) while (*) Nacib Saad begins an affair with his cook. In another novel from this country, a boy travels across the Sahara on the advice of the King of Salem in order to achieve his Personal Legend. This country is home to the authors of The Alchemist and Gabriela, Clove and Cinnamon. For 10 points, name this South American country home to writers like Paulo Coelho and Jorge Amado, who wrote in Portuguese.
tossups.answer: Federative Republic of Brazil [or República Federativa do Brasil]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Federative Republic of Brazil [or Republica Federativa do Brasil]
tossups.question_sanitized: In a work from this country, the image of the Emperor's black beard is repeated in a poem that summarizes the desire of the virgin Ofenisia. In a novel from this country, the Bastos ("BAH-stoose") clan and Mundinho Falcao ("moon-JEEN-yoo fao-COW") struggle for control of the town of Ilheus ("eel-YEH-oos") while (*) Nacib Saad begins an affair with his cook. In another novel from this country, a boy travels across the Sahara on the advice of the King of Salem in order to achieve his Personal Legend. This country is home to the authors of The Alchemist and Gabriela, Clove and Cinnamon. For 10 points, name this South American country home to writers like Paulo Coelho and Jorge Amado, who wrote in Portuguese.

tossups.question: The protagonist of a novel of this name has his pouch of savings stolen by a man who had agreed to purchase them animals in a marketplace in a section whose thematic phrase translates as “It is written.” A debate in a play with this name is settled with the declaration that casting coins does not count as illegal counterfeiting. It is the name of a novel in which the protagonist works for a crystal merchant to obtain money for a trip across the (*) Sahara, where he hopes to fulfill his personal legend. A novel with this name ends with Santiago discovering treasure buried under a sycamore tree, and a play with this name ends with Lovewit marrying Dame Pliant after the scams of Face and Subtle backfire. For 10 points, identity this shared title of a Paulo Coelho novel and a Ben Jonson play.
tossups.question_sanitized: The protagonist of a novel of this name has his pouch of savings stolen by a man who had agreed to purchase them animals in a marketplace in a section whose thematic phrase translates as "It is written." It is the name of a novel in which the protagonist works for a crystal merchant to obtain money for a trip across the (*) Sahara, where he hopes to fulfill his personal legend. A novel with this name ends with Santiago discovering treasure buried under a sycamore tree, and a play with this name ends with Lovewit marrying Dame Pliant after the scams of Face and Subtle backfire. For 10 points, identity this shared title of a Paulo Coelho novel and a Ben Jonson play.

tossups.question: In this novel, the protagonist sees one hawk attacking another, causing a vision of his oasis coming under siege. That protagonist of this novel was later released from a militant camp after turning himself into the wind. The protagonist tells a (+) gypsy palm reader about his recurring dream of hidden treasure at the Egyptian pyramids before meeting Melchizedek, the King of Salem, who gives him black and white stones for reading omens called (*) Urim and Thummim. The protagonist works for a crystal merchant in Tangier after selling his flock in Andalusia and before joining a caravan to cross the Sahara. For ten points, name this novel in which the title character teaches Santiago how to fulfill his Personal Legend written by Paulo Coelho.
tossups.question_sanitized: In this novel, the protagonist sees one hawk attacking another, causing a vision of his oasis coming under siege. That protagonist of this novel was later released from a militant camp after turning himself into the wind. The protagonist tells a (+) gypsy palm reader about his recurring dream of hidden treasure at the Egyptian pyramids before meeting Melchizedek, the King of Salem, who gives him black and white stones for reading omens called (*) Urim and Thummim. The protagonist works for a crystal merchant in Tangier after selling his flock in Andalusia and before joining a caravan to cross the Sahara. For ten points, name this novel in which the title character teaches Santiago how to fulfill his Personal Legend written by Paulo Coelho.

tossups.question: An important non-fiction work details this country’s Rebellion in the Backlands led by Antonio the Counselor and was written by Euclides da Cunha [koon-yuh]. The enchanting cooking of the title female for her Syrian-born husband Nacib Saad forms part of the plot of Gabriela, Clove, and Cinnamon, a novel written by an author from this country. Jorge Amado’s first novel argued that this country’s emphasis on the pre-Lenten festival hampered its development and is titled The Country of Carnival. For 10 points what South American country is home to Paulo Coelho, who wrote The Alchemist in Portuguese?
tossups.question_sanitized: An important non-fiction work details this country's Rebellion in the Backlands led by Antonio the Counselor and was written by Euclides da Cunha [koon-yuh]. The enchanting cooking of the title female for her Syrian-born husband Nacib Saad forms part of the plot of Gabriela, Clove, and Cinnamon, a novel written by an author from this country. Jorge Amado's first novel argued that this country's emphasis on the pre-Lenten festival hampered its development and is titled The Country of Carnival. For 10 points what South American country is home to Paulo Coelho, who wrote The Alchemist in Portuguese?

tossups.question: This leader, whose wife called him the “bald rat,” was known as the “little monkey” during his service as a sandal bearer. After an exchange with Gaspar Coelho in which he questioned the persecution of monks, this leader abolished slavery in his country. This leader attempted to ensure his family’s succession by setting up the Council of Five Elders and ordered the killing of twenty-six martyrs in Nagasaki. The Battle of Yamazaki was the defeat of Akechi Mitsuhide by this leader, who placed his son in Osaka Castle for safety. Admiral Yi Sun-Sin repulsed this leader’s disastrous invasion of Korea. For 10 points, name this second of the three Sengoku-era unifiers of Japan.
tossups.question_sanitized: This leader, whose wife called him the "bald rat," was known as the "little monkey" during his service as a sandal bearer. After an exchange with Gaspar Coelho in which he questioned the persecution of monks, this leader abolished slavery in his country. This leader attempted to ensure his family's succession by setting up the Council of Five Elders and ordered the killing of twenty-six martyrs in Nagasaki. The Battle of Yamazaki was the defeat of Akechi Mitsuhide by this leader, who placed his son in Osaka Castle for safety. Admiral Yi Sun-Sin repulsed this leader's disastrous invasion of Korea. For 10 points, name this second of the three Sengoku-era unifiers of Japan.

tossups.question: This book’s second chapter discusses a student in a French class who delivers a 45-minute talk about how to make fish soup to satisfy an assignment that he speak for 5 minutes, illustrating the culturally significant phase “to speak of all kind of things.” In a chapter on “The Eight Questions,” this book describes how two people would likely say that Yer’s slamming of a door frightened another person’s soul out of her body, necessitating a txiv neeb to sacrifice pigs and chickens. It mentions Congressman Tony Coelho and the characteristics of a good shaman in a discussion of its title phrase, a translation of qaug dab peg. This book, the bulk of which is set in Merced, California, describes growing mistrust between Neil Ernst and Peggy Philip, and Foua Yang and Na Kao Lee, over the treatment of Lia Lee’s epilepsy. For 10 points, name this work of medical anthropology in which Anne Fadiman chronicles “a Hmong child, her American doctors, and the collision of two cultures.”
tossups.answer_sanitized: The Spirit Catches You and You Fall Down
tossups.question_sanitized: This book's second chapter discusses a student in a French class who delivers a 45-minute talk about how to make fish soup to satisfy an assignment that he speak for 5 minutes, illustrating the culturally significant phase "to speak of all kind of things." It mentions Congressman Tony Coelho and the characteristics of a good shaman in a discussion of its title phrase, a translation of qaug dab peg. This book, the bulk of which is set in Merced, California, describes growing mistrust between Neil Ernst and Peggy Philip, and Foua Yang and Na Kao Lee, over the treatment of Lia Lee's epilepsy. For 10 points, name this work of medical anthropology in which Anne Fadiman chronicles "a Hmong child, her American doctors, and the collision of two cultures."

bonuses.leadin: In a story by this author, the sinologist Dr. Albert is killed in his study after discovering that the word “time” never appears in a book. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In a story by this author, the sinologist Dr. Albert is killed in his study after discovering that the word "time" never appears in a book. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Argentine author who wrote about hexagonal rooms that contain every possible book in “The Library of Babel.”
The discovery that a book never uses the word “time” is made in this Borges story, wherein the title timeless structure is found to be that novel by Ts’ui Pên.

One of the Library of Babel’s few readable sentences addresses “O time,” attributing these structures to it. Dreams of being led to these structures cause Santiago to go on a journey in a Paulo Coelho (“co-EL-yo”) novel.
parts_sanitized: Name this Argentine author who wrote about hexagonal rooms that contain every possible book in "The Library of Babel."
The discovery that a book never uses the word "time" is made in this Borges story, wherein the title timeless structure is found to be that novel by Ts'ui Pen.

One of the Library of Babel's few readable sentences addresses "O time," attributing these structures to it. Dreams of being led to these structures cause Santiago to go on a journey in a Paulo Coelho ("co-EL-yo") novel.
answers: “The Garden of Forking Paths” [or “El jardín de senderos que se bifurcan”]
answers_sanitized: "The Garden of Forking Paths" [or "El jardin de senderos que se bifurcan"]
pyramids [accept "O time, thy pyramids" or "Oh, tiempo, tus piramides"]

packet.name: A - Brown A + UBC A + Chicago B + Edinburgh B + UCSD A + Lancaster A + Toronto A

bonuses.leadin: A playwright with this surname used a multistoried stage to simultaneously depict different time periods in plays like The Path of Salvation and The Moratorium. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this surname shared by two members of the Group of Five: one who wrote about a shapeshifting man in Macunaíma and another whose wife Tarsila do Amaral inspired his Anthropophagist Manifesto.
The Grupo Dos Cinco hailed from this home country of authors such as Clarice Lispector, Machado de Assis, and Paulo Coelho.

A line from the Anthropophagist Manifesto parodies this quote with a Brazilian tribe’s name. This six word quote opens the nunnery scene and is followed by “Whether 'tis nobler in the mind to suffer/The slings and arrows of outrageous fortune.”
answers: Andrade [accept Jorge Andrade; accept Mário de Andrade; accept Oswald de Andrade]
“To be, or not to be” (The parody line is “Tupi, or not Tupi”)
answers_sanitized: Andrade [accept Jorge Andrade; accept Mario de Andrade; accept Oswald de Andrade]
"To be, or not to be" (The parody line is "Tupi, or not Tupi")

A line from the Anthropophagist Manifesto parodies this quote with a Brazilian tribe's name. This six word quote opens the nunnery scene and is followed by "Whether 'tis nobler in the mind to suffer/The slings and arrows of outrageous fortune."

bonuses.leadin: The protagonist of this novel turns himself into a dust storm while travelling through a volatile region en route to the pyramids. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Portuguese-language 1988 novel in which a fortune-teller tells Santiago to go to Egypt to find buried treasure, only for him to discover that the treasure is in the exact spot in Spain he started in.
The author of The Alchemist , Paulo Coelho, is one of the many Portuguese-speaking authors from this South American country.

Other fortune-tellers in Brazilian literature include Madame Carlota, who tells the protagonist of this novel that she will meet a wealthy foreigner only for her to be run over immediately by a yellow car.
answers: The Hour of the Star [or A hora da estrela ] (by Clarice Lispector)
answers_sanitized: The  Hour of the Star [or  A  hora da estrela ] (by Clarice Lispector)

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, name these characters that appear in the two works The Alchemist :
parts: In Coelho’s The Alchemist , this character gives Santiago the mystical stones Urim and Thummim. The Biblical character of this name was the king of Salem and names the order of the priesthood to which Jesus was inducted.

In Jonson’s The Alchemist , Subtle and Face tell Dapper that he will have to consult this mythical character in order to obtain luck in gambling, which of course also involves having Dapper hand over lots of money.
answers_sanitized: Queen of Fairy [prompt on partial answer]

bonuses.leadin: This author wrote about depressed Slovenian woman in his 1998 novel Veronika Decides to Die. For 10 points each:
answers: Paulo Coelho [“ko-EY-lyoh”, though a phonetic English pronunciation is fine too]
an autobiography [or memoir; accept The Posthumous Memoirs of Bras Cubas or Memórias Póstumas de Brás Cubas]
parts: Name this contemporary novelist who wrote of an Andalusian author who journeys to Egypt in search of treasure in his 1988 novel The Alchemist.
Coelho wrote in this language, which his Brazilian countryman Jorge Amado used to write Tieta the Goat Girl and Gabriela, Clove and Cinnamon.

Fellow Brazilian author Joaquim Machado de Assis wrote a fictional work in this genre narrated by the deceased Bras Cubas. Mark Twain’s work in this genre was published in 2013, 103 years after his death.
answers_sanitized: Paulo Coelho ["ko-EY-lyoh", though a phonetic English pronunciation is fine too]
Portuguese language [or portugues; or lingua portuguesa]
an autobiography [or memoir; accept The Posthumous Memoirs of Bras Cubas or Memorias Postumas de Bras Cubas]

bonuses.leadin: In one play by this author, The Comedy of Bristo, or The Pimp, Alexander betrays his friend Leonardo after he sees Camille’s beauty, but the help of the title character and Pindaro’s timely arrival allow the two to wed. F10PE:
parts: Name this Portuguese poet, who also wrote a tragedy in which Coelho and Pacheco execute the title lover of Pedro, despite that woman winning clemency from Pedro’s father Alphonso IV, The Tragedy of Ines de Castro.

Ferreira based the lecherous Hannibal, who regularly reminds the parasite Montalvam of his exploits in Rhodes and who tries to purchase Camille from Bristo, on Pyrgopolynices, the title braggart solider of this Plautine comedy.

The story of the historical Ines de Castro was also tackled in the novel Agnes of Castro by this member of the “fair triumvirate of wit”, who also wrote the Restoration Comedies The Feigned Courtesans and The Rover.
answers: Miles Gloriosus [do not accept The Braggart Soldier or logical English equivalents]
answers_sanitized: Miles Gloriosus [do not accept The Braggart Soldier or logical English equivalents]
